title: chrysobalanus icaco var. pellocarpus
intro: Chrysobalanus icaco var. pellocarpus, also known as the West Indian or Mayan jocote, is a variety of the icaco tree, native to Central America and the Caribbean. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Chrysobalanus icaco var. pellocarpus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the fruit, bark, and leaves of the plant are prepared in various ways, including as a tea, decoction, or tincture, to treat a range of health conditions.
